name: Encore — Capabilities-as-Regions for Active Objects
summary: Brandauer, Castegren, Clarke, Wrigstad et al.'s Encore (UpScale, 2015–2018) is an actor/active-object language whose type system uses reference capabilities (linear, locally-shared, subordinate, thread-local, read-only) to control aliasing across active-object boundaries. Per-actor unshared local heaps + capability-checked transfer make it the cleanest example of 'capabilities-as-regions' at the language level.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Brandauer, Castegren, Clarke, Wrigstad et al.'s **Encore** (UpScale, 2015–2018) is an actor/active-object language whose type system uses reference capabilities (linear, locally-shared, subordinate, thread-local, read-only) to control aliasing across active-object boundaries. A capability annotates a *type*, demarcating an aliasing regime — ownership is checked structurally rather than via Tofte-Talpin region variables.

Encore is the cleanest example of "capabilities-as-regions" at the language level — closer in shape to Pony than to Cyclone, but with a stronger emphasis on parallel-combinator coordination. Status (as of 2026-04): the UpScale project ended; Encore is no longer actively developed as a vehicle, but its capability vocabulary heavily influenced later work.

sections.title: Per-actor heaps and capability-checked transfer
sections.content:
Per-actor unshared local heaps + capability-checked transfer: passing an exclusive (linear) capability to another actor is a **move**; subordinate objects cannot escape their owning actor; thread-local capabilities cannot be sent at all. This means the runtime can keep per-actor allocators independent and free of cross-actor pointers, modulo controlled transfer points.

The capability lattice (linear, locally-shared, subordinate, thread-local, read-only) covers most practical aliasing regimes: linear for moves, locally-shared for read-only sharing within an actor, subordinate for owned helpers, thread-local for non-transferable state, and read-only for shared immutable data. Each capability rules out a specific class of aliasing race.

sections.title: Influence on later capability designs
sections.content:
Encore directly influenced **Pony** (the same capability lattice idea, packaged into a production-aimed language) and **Verona** (per-region capabilities with active-region windowing, see `data/verona`). The Boyapati single-owner RTSJ regions work (`data/boyapati-rtsj-regions`) is the older cousin in the "regions + ownership types" tradition; Encore is its actor-flavoured descendant.

For a language designer choosing between Tofte-Talpin region variables and capability-as-region, Encore demonstrates that capability-typing scales to OO/actor-style programs without the inference complexity of region-polymorphism, at the cost of more annotations on shared types.
